creator: fortin@acm.org
text:
Upgraded to the latest snapshot last night.  After 12 hours
		of operation, the list of routes on our gateway has grown
		to contain a ton and a half of lines (I was going to include 
		the output of "netstat -rn", but the machine has just crashed)!

		Has something major changed in the handling of routes?  (I used
		the same configuration file for gated that I used to build it
		under 2.0)

Fix: 

I've just encountered this; I haven't had the time to 
		search for a fix.  I might try to revert to good old (?)
		routed, and see if that helps, but I'm not convinced
		(cf: bmk@dtr.com's problems with routed).

		Any suggestions?
How-To-Repeat: 		Let the system run for a while; type "netstat -r".  Notice
		how long the list of machines is; worry that the non-removal
		of old routes may be what causing login problems for folks
		running SLIP.
